medications.indication_rationale: Definitive management of a fluctuant, pointing abscess; must be done in person (cannot be performed by telehealth or by the patient).
For the surrounding cellulitis and systemic features; adjunct to drainage, not a substitute.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 500 mg orally four times daily for ~7 days (as per source note; confirm at prescriber review)
contraindications_in_this_case: Penicillin allergy — use an alternative (e.g., clindamycin/cephalexin) if allergic

medications.indication_rationale: Squeezing or lancing at home risks spreading infection and bacteraemia; drainage must be done by a clinician.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: After drainage, apply warm compresses over the dressing and keep the wound clean and dry; wash hands before/after touching the area.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Supports drainage/healing and prevents auto-inoculation to other sites.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Take the antibiotic as prescribed and complete the course.

patient_education_points.point: This is a boil that has formed a pocket of pus. The main treatment is to have it drained by a clinician today — antibiotics alone won't fix a pus pocket.
Don't squeeze or lance it yourself — that can spread the infection into your bloodstream.

If the redness spreads, your fever goes above 39°C, you feel confused or dizzy, or you develop trouble swallowing, drooling, a voice change or difficulty breathing — go to the emergency department / call 000.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Wound/drainage cavity, dressing change, resolution of cellulitis and systemic features.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Spreading redness, high fever/confusion, or any swallowing/voice/breathing change — seek emergency care.

must_include_items: Recognise a fluctuant, pointing abscess requiring incision and drainage
Arrange urgent same-day in-person drainage (not achievable via telehealth)
Oral anti-staphylococcal antibiotic for the surrounding cellulitis
acceptable_alternatives: Wound swab to guide therapy / exclude MRSA
errors_of_commission: Advising the patient to squeeze/lance it at home
Managing entirely remotely with antibiotics and no in-person drainage
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires arranging in-person incision and drainage PLUS antibiotics for cellulitis, with sepsis and airway/deep-neck safety-netting. Antibiotics-only management is a key failure.
